title: hieracium alpinum subsp. halleri
intro: Hieracium alpinum subsp. halleri, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and prevent infection. The health benefits of Alpine Hawkweed include its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and boost the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Alpine Hawkweed can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
